headlines.title: Netflix's stock split history (& prospects) explained
headlines.summary: Netflix revolutionized home cinematic viewing, first with its DVD subscription service, then with the rollout of its online streaming service.

headlines.title: Here Is Why You Should Buy Netflix And Roku
headlines.summary: Netflix and Roku just reported earnings that could not look more different, and understanding that gap reveals a split-ticket streaming trade worth considering before Roku's next report drops on July 30.

headlines.title: The Big Question Hanging Over Netflix Stock
headlines.summary: With the stock already under pressure, the biggest risk for investors is a fundamental shift in how the company measures success, just as its growth is visibly slowing.

headlines.title: Comcast's Peacock records first ever profit on World Cup, 'Love Island USA' boost
headlines.summary: Comcast's Peacock streaming service reported its first quarterly profit on Thursday, as the soccer World Cup and the hit reality ‌show "Love Island USA" attracted more subscribers.  The $189 million pre-tax profit marks a major win ‌for the streaming service.  The service added 2 million paid subscribers in the April-June quarter, nearly four times the figure expected by analysts who were polled by Visible Alpha.

headlines.title: Fox Buys Roku, and Now Comcast Is Spinning Off NBCUniversal. Does Netflix Need to Make a Big Move This Summer?
headlines.summary: Netflix just filed some intriguing SEC documents. Spoiler alert: It's not another megadeal.

description: Netflix, Inc. provides entertainment services worldwide. The company offers television (TV) series, documentaries, feature films, games, and live programming across various genres and languages. It also provides members the ability to receive streaming content through a host of internet-connected devices, including TVs, digital video players, TV set-top boxes, and mobile devices. Netflix, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Los Gatos, California.
